title: Lift systems and methods for launching and recovering structures in a marine environment
abstract: Systems and methods for assembling, launching, retrieving, and maintaining floating wind foundations including tower, nacelle, and blades with two-way operability are disclosed. The systems include a dock having a lift platform and a first plurality of chain jacks configured to lift and lower the lift platform. The lift platform has a width and length with a span sufficient to receive, lower, and lift a floating wind foundation. The lift platform includes an upper deck and a plurality of box truss girders supporting the upper deck. The box truss girders extend across the span of the width of the lift platform and are of sufficient strength to support a floating wind foundation.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A lift system for launch and recovery of marine equipment, the lift system comprising: a lift platform, wherein the lift platform has a width of at least sixty meters and a length of at least sixty meters, and wherein the lift platform comprises an upper deck supported by a plurality of girders; a plurality of lifts, wherein the lifts are coupled with the lift platform and configured to raise and lower the lift platform. 2. The lift system of claim 1 , wherein the girders extend across an entirety of the width of the lift platform. 3. The lift system of claim 1, wherein the lift platform has a shape and size configured to accommodate foundations of wind turbines. 4. The lift system of claim 1, wherein the length and width of the lift platform are equal such that the upper deck has a square shape. 5. The lift system of claim 1 , comprising two lift platforms, wherein each lift platform is coupled with a plurality of lifts. 6. The lift system of claim 1, wherein the width of the upper deck ranges from sixty meters to one hundred twenty meters, and wherein the length of the upper deck ranges from sixty meters to one hundred twenty meters. 7. The lift system of claim 1, wherein each girder comprises a lower truss, an upper truss, and a plurality of web trusses extending between the lower and upper trusses. 8. The lift system of claim 1, wherein the girders comprise box truss girders. 9. The lift system of claim 1, wherein the girders comprises steel. 10. The lift system of claim 1, further comprising a buoyancy tank coupled with the lift platform. 11. The lift system of claim 10, wherein the buoyancy tank provides the lift platform with a negative buoyancy. 12. The lift system of claim 10, wherein the buoyancy tank is positioned along a central axis of the lift platform. 13. The lift system of claim 10, wherein the buoyancy tank offsets dead load of the lift platform. 14. The lift system of claim 1, wherein the lifts comprise chain jacks. 15. The lift system of claim 14, wherein each chain jack includes a chain on a chainwheel and a cylinder jack coupled with the chain, wherein the chain is coupled with the lift platform, and wherein the cylinder jack is actuable to release the chain to lower the lift platform or haul-in the chain to raise the lift platform. 16. The lift system of claim 15, further comprising a hydraulic power unit coupled with the chain jacks and configured to actuate the cylinder jacks. 17. The lift system of claim 15, further comprising one or more plates coupled between the chain and the lift platform. 18. The lift system of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of rails on the lift platform to accommodate bogies for transport of marine equipment along the rails. 19. A method of deploying and retrieving marine equipment, the method comprising: positioning marine equipment on a lift platform, wherein the lift platform has a width of at least sixty meters and a length of at least sixty meters, and wherein the lift platform comprises an upper deck supported by a plurality of girders; raising the lift platform with a plurality of lifts to retrieve the marine equipment, or lowering the lift platform with the plurality of lifts to deploy the marine equipment. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the marine equipment comprises an offshore wind turbine or components thereof, a ship or components thereof, a submarine or components thereof, or yacht or components thereof. 21. The method of claim 19, wherein the marine equipment comprises a semisubmersible floating wind foundation or a tension leg platform floating wind foundations. 22. The method of claim 19, wherein positioning the marine equipment on the lift platform comprises transporting the marine equipment onto the lift platform with bogies on rails. 23. The method of claim 19, further comprising providing a negative buoyancy to the lift platform using a buoyancy tank to offset dead load of the lift platform. 24. The method of claim 19, wherein the method is performed using a lift system in accordance with any of claims 1 to 18. 25. A floating dock for deploying and retrieving marine equipment, the floating dock comprising: a dock; a plurality of lifts on the dock; a lift platform positioned adjacent the dock, wherein the lift platform has a width of at least sixty meters and a length of at least sixty meters, and wherein the lift platform comprises an upper deck supported by a plurality of girders; wherein the lifts are coupled with the lift platform and configured to raise and lower the lift platform relative to the dock. 26. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the girders extend across an entirety of the width of the lift platform. 27. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the lift platform has a shape and size configured to accommodate foundations of wind turbines. 28. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the length and width of the lift platform are equal such that the upper deck has a square shape. 29. The floating dock of claim 25, comprising two lift platforms, wherein each lift platform is coupled with a plurality of lifts on the dock. 30. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the width of the upper deck ranges from sixty meters to one hundred twenty meters, and wherein the length of the upper deck ranges from sixty meters to one hundred twenty meters. 31. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein each girder comprises a lower truss, an upper truss, and a plurality of web trusses extending between the lower and upper trusses. 32. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the girders comprise box truss girders. 33. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the girders comprises steel. 34. The floating dock of claim 25, further comprising a buoyancy tank coupled with the lift platform. 35. The floating dock of claim 34, wherein the buoyancy tank is configured to provide the lift platform with a negative buoyancy. 36. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the buoyancy tank is positioned along a central axis of the lift platform. 37. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the buoyancy tank offsets dead load of the lift platform. 38. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the lifts are positioned on the dock along two sides of the lift platform. 39. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the lifts comprise chain jacks. 40. The floating dock of claim 39, wherein each chain jack includes a chain on a chainwheel and a cylinder jack coupled with the chain, wherein the chain is coupled with the lift platform, and wherein the cylinder jack is actuable to release the chain to lower the lift platform or haul-in the chain to raise the lift platform. 41. The floating dock of claim 40, further comprising a hydraulic power unit on the dock, wherein the hydraulic power unit is coupled with the chain jacks and configured to actuate the cylinder jacks. 42. The floating dock of claim 40, further comprising one or more plates coupled between the chain and the lift platform. 43. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the lifts are configured to raise and lower the lift platform between a first height above a water level and a second height below the water level. 44. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the dock at least partially defines a space, and wherein the lift platform is positioned at least partially within the space. 45. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the dock is moored at a site. 46. The floating dock of claim 45, wherein the site is in proximity to a wind farm. 47. The floating dock of claim 25, wherein the dock comprises a dry dock space for storage of components of marine equipment and an assembly area for assembly of marine equipment. 48. The floating dock of claim 47, wherein the marine equipment comprises foundations, towers, nacelles, and blades for offshore wind turbines. 49. The floating dock of claim 25, further comprising a plurality of rails on the dock and the lift platform, and a plurality of bogies for transport of marine equipment along the rails on the dock and lift platform. 50. A wind farm system, the system comprising: a wind farm positioned at a first offshore site, the wind farm comprises a plurality of offshore wind turbines; a floating dock moored at a second offshore site, wherein the second offshore site is in close proximity to the first offshore site; a plurality of lifts on the floating dock; a lift platform positioned adjacent the floating dock, wherein the lift platform has a width of at least sixty meters and a length of at least sixty meters, and wherein the lift platform comprises an upper deck supported by a plurality of girders; wherein the lifts are coupled with the lift platform and configured to raise and lower the lift platform relative to the floating dock. 51. The system of claim 50, further comprising one or more tug boats configured for transport of wind turbines or components thereof between the first and second offshore sites. 52. The system of claim 50, wherein the floating dock is a floating dock in accordance with any of claims 25 to 49. 53. A method of assembling, maintaining, and decommissioning a wind farm, the method comprising: mooring a floating dock at a first offshore site; assembling an offshore wind turbine or components thereof on the floating dock; deploying the offshore wind turbine or components thereof into water at the first offshore site, wherein the deploying comprises positioning the offshore wind turbine or components thereof on a lift platform of the floating dock and lowering the lift platform into the water; and transporting the offshore wind turbine or components thereof from the first offshore site to a second offshore site and installing the offshore wind turbine or components thereof at the second offshore site to form a wind farm at the second offshore site. 54. The method of claim 53, further comprising: retrieving an offshore wind turbine or components thereof from the second offshore site; 

 transporting the offshore wind turbine or components thereof from the second offshore site to the first offshore site; retrieving the offshore wind turbine or components thereof from the water at the first offshore site, wherein the retrieving comprises positioning the offshore wind turbine or components thereof on the lift platform and raising the lift platform out of the water; and repairing, maintaining, or decommissioning the offshore wind turbine or components thereof. 55. The method of claim 53, wherein the assembling comprises assembling a foundation of an offshore wind turbine. 56. The method of claim 55, wherein the assembling comprises assembling a tower, nacelle, and blades onto the foundation. 57. The method of claim 53, wherein the transporting comprises towing using one or more tug boats. 58. The method of claim 53, wherein the floating dock is a floating dock in accordance with any of claims 25 to 49.
